questions.questionText: Let S = {1, 2, 3, 4}. Then the number of elements in the set { f : S $$\times$$ S $$\to$$ S : f is onto and f (a, b) = f (b, a) $$\ge$$ a $$\forall$$ (a, b) $$\in$$ S $$\times$$ S } is ______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: When sugar 'X' is boiled with dilute H2SO4 in alcoholic solution, two isomers 'A' and 'B' are formed. 'A' on oxidation with HNO3 yields saccharic acid where as 'B' is laevorotatory. The compound 'X' is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: If n arithmetic means are inserted between a and 100 such that the ratio of the first mean to the last mean is 1 : 7 and a + n = 33, then the value of n is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let a triangle be bounded by the lines L1 : 2x + 5y = 10; L2 : $$-$$4x + 3y = 12 and the line L3, which passes through the point P(2, 3), intersects L2 at A and L1 at B. If the point P divides the line-segment AB, internally in the ratio 1 : 3, then the area of the triangle is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let f(x) be a quadratic polynomial such that f($$-$$2) + f(3) = 0. If one of the roots of f(x) = 0 is $$-$$1, then the sum of the roots of f(x) = 0 is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The area of the bounded region enclosed by the curve

$$y = 3 - \left| {x - {1 \over 2}} \right| - |x + 1|$$ and the x-axis is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let x = x(y) be the solution of the differential equation

$$2y\,{e^{x/{y^2}}}dx + \left( {{y^2} - 4x{e^{x/{y^2}}}} \right)dy = 0$$ such that x(1) = 0. Then, x(e) is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The probability that a randomly chosen one-one function from the set {a, b, c, d} to the set {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} satisfies f(a) + 2f(b) $$-$$ f(c) = f(d) is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of ways to distribute 30 identical candies among four children C1, C2, C3 and C4 so that C2 receives at least 4 and at most 7 candies, C3 receives at least 2 and at most 6 candies, is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The value of

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } 6\tan \left\{ {\sum\limits_{r = 1}^n {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {{1 \over {{r^2} + 3r + 3}}} \right)} } \right\}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let R1 = {(a, b) $$\in$$ N $$\times$$ N : |a $$-$$ b| $$\le$$ 13} and

R2 = {(a, b) $$\in$$ N $$\times$$ N : |a $$-$$ b| $$\ne$$ 13}. Then on N :
options: (A) Both R<sub>1</sub> and R<sub>2</sub> are equivalence relations
(B) Neither R<sub>1</sub> nor R<sub>2</sub> is an equivalence relation
(C) R<sub>1</sub> is an equivalence relation but R<sub>2</sub> is not
(D) R<sub>2</sub> is an equivalence relation but R<sub>1</sub> is not
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let f : R $$\to$$ R be a continuous function satisfying f(x) + f(x + k) = n, for all x $$\in$$ R where k > 0 and n is a positive integer. If $${I_1} = \int\limits_0^{4nk} {f(x)dx} $$ and $${I_2} = \int\limits_{ - k}^{3k} {f(x)dx} $$, then :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let f, g : R $$\to$$ R be functions defined by

$$f(x) = \left\{ {\matrix{ {[x]} & , & {x < 0} \cr {|1 - x|} & , & {x \ge 0} \cr } } \right.$$ and $$g(x) = \left\{ {\matrix{ {{e^x} - x} & , & {x < 0} \cr {{{(x - 1)}^2} - 1} & , & {x \ge 0} \cr } } \right.$$ where [x] denote the greatest integer less than or equal to x. Then, the function fog is discontinuous at exactly :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The term independent of x in the expansion of

$$(1 - {x^2} + 3{x^3}){\left( {{5 \over 2}{x^3} - {1 \over {5{x^2}}}} \right)^{11}},\,x \ne 0$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If cot$$\alpha$$ = 1 and sec$$\beta$$ = $$ - {5 \over 3}$$, where $$\pi < \alpha < {{3\pi } \over 2}$$ and $${\pi \over 2} < \beta < \pi $$, then the value of $$\tan (\alpha + \beta )$$ and the quadrant in which $$\alpha$$ + $$\beta$$ lies, respectively are :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\overrightarrow a = \alpha \widehat i + 2\widehat j - \widehat k$$ and $$\overrightarrow b = - 2\widehat i + \alpha \widehat j + \widehat k$$, where $$\alpha \in R$$. If the area of the parallelogram whose adjacent sides are represented by the vectors $$\overrightarrow a $$ and $$\overrightarrow b $$ is $$\sqrt {15({\alpha ^2} + 4)} $$, then the value of $$2{\left| {\overrightarrow a } \right|^2} + \left( {\overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow b } \right){\left| {\overrightarrow b } \right|^2}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If vertex of a parabola is (2, $$-$$1) and the equation of its directrix is 4x $$-$$ 3y = 21, then the length of its latus rectum is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let a > 0, b > 0. Let e and l respectively be the eccentricity and length of the latus rectum of the hyperbola $${{{x^2}} \over {{a^2}}} - {{{y^2}} \over {{b^2}}} = 1$$. Let e' and l' respectively be the eccentricity and length of the latus rectum of its conjugate hyperbola. If $${e^2} = {{11} \over {14}}l$$ and $${\left( {e'} \right)^2} = {{11} \over 8}l'$$, then the value of $$77a + 44b$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\overrightarrow a $$ be a vector which is perpendicular to the vector $$3\widehat i + {1 \over 2}\widehat j + 2\widehat k$$. If $$\overrightarrow a \times \left( {2\widehat i + \widehat k} \right) = 2\widehat i - 13\widehat j - 4\widehat k$$, then the projection of the vector $$\overrightarrow a $$ on the vector $$2\widehat i + 2\widehat j + \widehat k$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the slope of the tangent to a curve y = f(x) at (x, y) be given by 2 $$\tan x(\cos x - y)$$. If the curve passes through the point $$\left( {{\pi \over 4},0} \right)$$, then the value of $$\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {y\,dx} $$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Sum of squares of modulus of all the complex numbers z satisfying $$\overline z = i{z^2} + {z^2} - z$$ is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If one of the diameters of the circle $${x^2} + {y^2} - 2\sqrt 2 x - 6\sqrt 2 y + 14 = 0$$ is a chord of the circle $${(x - 2\sqrt 2 )^2} + {(y - 2\sqrt 2 )^2} = {r^2}$$, then the value of r2 is equal to ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let f : R $$\to$$ R be a differentiable function such that $$f\left( {{\pi \over 4}} \right) = \sqrt 2 ,\,f\left( {{\pi \over 2}} \right) = 0$$ and $$f'\left( {{\pi \over 2}} \right) = 1$$ and

let $$g(x) = \int_x^{\pi /4} {(f'(t)\sec t + \tan t\sec t\,f(t))\,dt} $$ for $$x \in \left[ {{\pi \over 4},{\pi \over 2}} \right)$$. Then $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {{\left( {{\pi \over 2}} \right)}^ - }} g(x)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the image of the point P(1, 2, 3) in the line $$L:{{x - 6} \over 3} = {{y - 1} \over 2} = {{z - 2} \over 3}$$ be Q. Let R ($$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$, $$\gamma$$) be a point that divides internally the line segment PQ in the ratio 1 : 3. Then the value of 22 ($$\alpha$$ + $$\beta$$ + $$\gamma$$) is equal to __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 1} {{\sin (3{x^2} - 4x + 1) - {x^2} + 1} \over {2{x^3} - 7{x^2} + ax + b}} = - 2$$, then the value of (a $$-$$ b) is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Suppose a class has 7 students. The average marks of these students in the mathematics examination is 62, and their variance is 20. A student fails in the examination if he/she gets less than 50 marks, then in worst case, the number of students can fail is _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$A = \left( {\matrix{ {1 + i} & 1 \cr { - i} & 0 \cr } } \right)$$ where $$i = \sqrt { - 1} $$. Then, the number of elements in the set { n $$\in$$ {1, 2, ......, 100} : An = A } is ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let for n = 1, 2, ......, 50, Sn be the sum of the infinite geometric progression whose first term is n2 and whose common ratio is $${1 \over {{{(n + 1)}^2}}}$$. Then the value of

$${1 \over {26}} + \sum\limits_{n = 1}^{50} {\left( {{S_n} + {2 \over {n + 1}} - n - 1} \right)} $$ is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Resistance of the wire is measured as 2 $$\Omega$$ and 3 $$\Omega$$ at 10$$^\circ$$C and 30$$^\circ$$C respectively. Temperature co-efficient of resistance of the material of the wire is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
If the system of linear equations
$$2x - 3y = \gamma + 5$$,
$$\alpha x + 5y = \beta + 1$$, where $$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$, $$\gamma$$ $$\in$$ R has infinitely many solutions then the value
of | 9$$\alpha$$ + 3$$\beta$$ + 5$$\gamma$$ | is equal to ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: In Young's double slit experiment performed using a monochromatic light of wavelength $$\lambda$$, when a glass plate ($$\mu$$ = 1.5) of thickness x$$\lambda$$ is introduced in the path of the one of the interfering beams, the intensity at the position where the central maximum occurred previously remains unchanged. The value of x will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two parallel, long wires are kept 0.20 m apart in vacuum, each carrying current of x A in the same direction. If the force of attraction per meter of each wire is 2 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$6 N, then the value of x is approximately :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An EM wave propagating in x-direction has a wavelength of 8 mm. The electric field vibrating y-direction has maximum magnitude of 60 Vm$$-$$1. Choose the correct equations for electric and magnetic fields if the EM wave is propagating in vacuum :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The space inside a straight current carrying solenoid is filled with a magnetic material having magnetic susceptibility equal to 1.2 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$5. What is fractional increase in the magnetic field inside solenoid with respect to air as medium inside the solenoid?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Let K1 and K2 be the maximum kinetic energies of photo-electrons emitted when two monochromatic beams of wavelength $$\lambda$$1 and $$\lambda$$2, respectively are incident on a metallic surface. If $$\lambda$$1 = 3$$\lambda$$2 then :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A coil is placed in a time varying magnetic field. If the number of turns in the coil were to be halved and the radius of wire doubled, the electrical power dissipated due to the current induced in the coil would be :

(Assume the coil to be short circuited.)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the given circuit the input voltage Vin is shown in figure. The cut-in voltage of p-n junction diode (D1 or D2) is 0.6 V. Which of the following output voltage (V0) waveform across the diode is correct?
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A block of mass 2 kg moving on a horizontal surface with speed of 4 ms$$-$$1 enters a rough surface ranging from x = 0.5 m to x = 1.5 m. The retarding force in this range of rough surface is related to distance by F = $$-$$kx where k = 12 Nm$$-$$1. The speed of the block as it just crosses the rough surface will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: What will be the effect on the root mean square velocity of oxygen molecules if the temperature is doubled and oxygen molecule dissociates into atomic oxygen?
options: (A) The velocity of atomic oxygen remains same
(B) The velocity of atomic oxygen doubles
(C) The velocity of atomic oxygen becomes half
(D) The velocity of atomic oxygen becomes four times
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two objects of equal masses placed at certain distance from each other attracts each other with a force of F. If one-third mass of one object is transferred to the other object, then the new force will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A sample of an ideal gas is taken through the cyclic process ABCA as shown in figure. It absorbs, 40 J of heat during the part AB, no heat during BC and rejects 60 J of heat during CA. A work of 50 J is done on the gas during the part BC. The internal energy of the gas at A is 1560 J. The workdone by the gas during the part CA is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A ball is spun with angular acceleration $$\alpha$$ = 6t2 $$-$$ 2t where t is in second and $$\alpha$$ is in rads$$-$$2. At t = 0, the ball has angular velocity of 10 rads$$-$$1 and angular position of 4 rad. The most appropriate expression for the angular position of the ball is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Water falls from a 40 m high dam at the rate of 9 $$\times$$ 104 kg per hour. Fifty percentage of gravitational potential energy can be converted into electrical energy. Using this hydroelectric energy number of 100 W lamps, that can be lit, is :

(Take g = 10 ms$$-$$2)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two point charges A and B of magnitude +8 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$6 C and $$-$$8 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$6 C respectively are placed at a distance d apart. The electric field at the middle point O between the charges is 6.4 $$\times$$ 104 NC$$-$$1. The distance 'd' between the point charges A and B is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A water drop of radius 1 $$\mu$$m falls in a situation where the effect of buoyant force is negligible. Co-efficient of viscosity of air is 1.8 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$5 Nsm$$-$$2 and its density is negligible as compared to that of water 106 gm$$-$$3. Terminal velocity of the water drop is :

(Take acceleration due to gravity = 10 ms$$-$$2)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A $$\sqrt {34} $$ m long ladder weighing 10 kg leans on a frictionless wall. Its feet rest on the floor 3 m away from the wall as shown in the figure. If Ef and Fw are the reaction forces of the floor and the wall, then ratio of $${F_w}/{F_f}$$ will be :

(Use g = 10 m/s2.)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A liquid of density 750 kgm$$-$$3 flows smoothly through a horizontal pipe that tapers in cross-sectional area from A1 = 1.2 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$2 m2 to A2 = $${{{A_1}} \over 2}$$. The pressure difference between the wide and narrow sections of the pipe is 4500 Pa. The rate of flow of liquid is ___________ $$\times$$ 10$$-$$3 m3s$$-$$1.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Velocity (v) and acceleration (a) in two systems of units 1 and 2 are related as $${v_2} = {n \over {{m^2}}}{v_1}$$ and $${a_2} = {{{a_1}} \over {mn}}$$ respectively. Here m and n are constants. The relations for distance and time in two systems respectively are :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A uniform disc with mass M = 4 kg and radius R = 10 cm is mounted on a fixed horizontal axle as shown in figure. A block with mass m = 2 kg hangs from a massless cord that is wrapped around the rim of the disc. During the fall of the block, the cord does not slip and there is no friction at the axle. The tension in the cord is ____________ N.

(Take g = 10 ms$$-$$2)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A car covers AB distance with first one-third at velocity v1 ms$$-$$1, second one-third at v2 ms$$-$$1 and last one-third at v3 ms$$-$$1. If v3 = 3v1, v2 = 2v1 and v1 = 11 ms$$-$$1 then the average velocity of the car is _____________ ms$$-$$1.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A zener of breakdown voltage Vz = 8 V and maximum zener current, IZM = 10 mA is subjectd to an input voltage Vi = 10 V with series resistance R = 100 $$\Omega$$. In the given circuit RL represents the variable load resistance. The ratio of maximum and minimum value of RL is _____________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A tunning fork of frequency 340 Hz resonates in the fundamental mode with an air column of length 125 cm in a cylindrical tube closed at one end. When water is slowly poured in it, the minimum height of water required for observing resonance once again is ___________ cm.

(Velocity of sound in air is 340 ms$$-$$1)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A capacitor C1 of capacitance 5 $$\mu$$F is charged to a potential of 30 V using a battery. The battery is then removed and the charged capacitor is connected to an uncharged capacitor C2 of capacitance 10 $$\mu$$F as shown in figure. When the switch is closed charge flows between the capacitors. At equilibrium, the charge on the capacitor C2 is __________ $$\mu$$C.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A student in the laboratory measures thickness of a wire using screw gauge. The readings are 1.22 mm, 1.23 mm, 1.19 mm and 1.20 mm. The percentage error is $${x \over {121}}\% $$. The value of x is ____________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: All resistances in figure are 1 $$\Omega$$ each. The value of current 'I' is $${a \over 5}$$ A. The value of a is _________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the given circuit, the magnitude of VL and VC are twice that of VR. Given that f = 50 Hz, the inductance of the coil is $${1 \over {K\pi }}$$ mH. The value of K is ____________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the structure of SF4, the lone pair of electrons on S is in.
options: (A) equatorial position and there are two lone pair - bond pair repulsions at 90$$^\circ$$.
(B) equatorial position and there are three lone pair - bond pair repulsions at 90$$^\circ$$.
(C) axial position and there are three lone pair - bond pair repulsion at 90$$^\circ$$.
(D) axial position and there are two lone pair - bond pair repulsion at 90$$^\circ$$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In a Young's double slit experiment, an angular width of the fringe is 0.35$$^\circ$$ on a screen placed at 2 m away for particular wavelength of 450 nm. The angular width of the fringe, when whole system is immersed in a medium of refractive index 7/5, is $${1 \over \alpha }$$. The value of $$\alpha$$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements :

(A) The principal quantum number 'n' is a positive integer with values of 'n' = 1, 2, 3, ...

(B) The azimuthal quantum number 'l' for a given 'n' (principal quantum number) can have values as 'l' = 0, 1, 2, ...... n

(C) Magnetic orbital quantum number 'ml' for a particular 'l' (azimuthal quantum number) has (2l + 1) values.

(D) $$\pm$$ 1/2 are the two possible orientations of electron spin.

(E) For l = 5, there will be a total of 9 orbital

Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Among the following, basic oxide is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The major product (P) of the given reaction is

(where, Me is $$-$$CH3)
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
With respect to the following reaction, consider the given statements :

(A) o-Nitroaniline and p-nitroaniline are the predominant products.

(B) p-Nitroaniline and m-nitroaniline are the predominant products.

(C) HNO3 acts as an acid.

(D) H2SO4 acts as an acid.

Choose the correct option.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In the above reaction 'A' is
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A student needs to prepare a buffer solution of propanoic acid and its sodium salt with pH 4. The ratio of $${{[C{H_3}C{H_2}CO{O^ - }]} \over {[C{H_3}C{H_2}COOH]}}$$ required to make buffer is ___________.

Given : $${K_a}(C{H_3}C{H_2}COOH) = 1.3 \times {10^{ - 5}}$$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Match List - I with List - II :

List-I (Oxide) List-II (Nature)
(A) $$C{l_2}{O_7}$$ (I) Amphoteric
(B) $$N{a_2}O$$ (II) Basic
(C) $$A{l_2}{O_3}$$ (III) Neutral
(D) $${N_2}O$$ (IV) Acidic

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Compound A contains 8.7% Hydrogen, 74% Carbon and 17.3% Nitrogen. The molecular formula of the compound is,

Given : Atomic masses of C, H and N are 12, 1 and 14 amu respectively.

The molar mass of the compound A is 162 g mol$$-$$1.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The correct IUPAC name of the following compound is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
2.5 g of protein containing only glycine (C2H5NO2) is dissolved in water to make 500 mL of solution. The osmotic pressure of this solution at 300 K is found to be 5.03 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$3 bar. The total number of glycine units present in the protein is ____________.

(Given : R = 0.083 L bar K$$-$$1 mol$$-$$1)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: 0.01 M KMnO4 solution was added to 20.0 mL of 0.05 M Mohr's salt solution through a burette. The initial reading of 50 mL burette is zero. The volume of KMnO4 solution left in the burette after the end point is _____________ mL. (nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A radioactive element has a half life of 200 days. The percentage of original activity remaining after 83 days is ___________. (Nearest integer)

(Given : antilog 0.125 = 1.333, antilog 0.693 = 4.93)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Isobutyraldehyde on reaction with formaldehyde and K2CO3 gives compound 'A'. Compound 'A' reacts with KCN and yields compound 'B', which on hydrolysis gives a stable compound 'C'. The compound 'C' is
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The complete combustion of 0.492 g of an organic compound containing 'C', 'H' and 'O' gives 0.793 g of CO2 and 0.442 g of H2O. The percentage of oxygen composition in the organic compound is ______________. (nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
(a) CoCl3.4NH3,    (b) CoCl3.5NH3,      (c) CoCl3.6NH3    and    (d) CoCl(NO3)2.5NH3.

Number of complex(es) which will exist in cis-trans form is/are _______________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
For the given reactions

Sn2+ + 2e$$-$$ $$\to$$ Sn

Sn4+ + 4e$$-$$ $$\to$$ Sn

the electrode potentials are ; $$E_{S{n^{2 + }}/Sn}^o = - 0.140$$ V and $$E_{S{n^{4 + }}/Sn}^o = + 0.010$$ V. The magnitude of standard electrode potential for $$S{n^{4 + }}/S{n^{2 + }}$$ i.e. $$E_{S{n^{4 + }}/S{n^{2 + }}}^o$$ is _____________ $$\times$$ 10$$-$$2 V. (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The major product of the following reaction contains ____________ bromine atom(s).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
For combustion of one mole of magnesium in an open container at 300 K and 1 bar pressure, $$\Delta$$CH$$\Theta $$ = $$-$$601.70 kJ mol$$-$$1, the magnitude of change in internal energy for the reaction is __________ kJ. (Nearest integer)

(Given : R = 8.3 J K$$-$$1 mol$$-$$1)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The maximum number of compound propositions, out of p$$\vee$$r$$\vee$$s, p$$\vee$$r$$\vee$$$$\sim$$s, p$$\vee$$$$\sim$$q$$\vee$$s, $$\sim$$p$$\vee$$$$\sim$$r$$\vee$$s, $$\sim$$p$$\vee$$$$\sim$$r$$\vee$$$$\sim$$s, $$\sim$$p$$\vee$$q$$\vee$$$$\sim$$s, q$$\vee$$r$$\vee$$$$\sim$$s, q$$\vee$$$$\sim$$r$$\vee$$$$\sim$$s, $$\sim$$p$$\vee$$$$\sim$$q$$\vee$$$$\sim$$s that can be made simultaneously true by an assignment of the truth values to p, q, r and s, is equal to __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let the plane ax + by + cz = d pass through (2, 3, $$-$$5) and is perpendicular to the planes
2x + y $$-$$ 5z = 10 and 3x + 5y $$-$$ 7z = 12. If a, b, c, d are integers d > 0 and gcd (|a|, |b|, |c|, d) = 1, then the value of a + 7b + c + 20d is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Amplitude modulated wave is represented by $${V_{AM}} = 10\,[1 + 0.4\cos (2\pi \times {10^4}t)]\cos (2\pi \times {10^7}t)$$. The total bandwidth of the amplitude modulated wave is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Following statements related to radioactivity are given below :

(A) Radioactivity is a random and spontaneous process and is dependent on physical and chemical conditions.

(B) The number of un-decayed nuclei in the radioactive sample decays exponentially with time.

(C) Slope of the graph of loge (no. of undecayed nuclei) Vs. time represents the reciprocal of mean life time ($$\tau$$).

(D) Product of decay constant ($$\lambda$$) and half-life time (T1/2) is not constant.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Match List - I with List - II :

List-I List-II
(A) negatively charged sol (I) $$F{e_2}{O_3}\,.\,x{H_2}O$$
(B) macromolecular colloid (II) CdS sol
(C) positively charged sol (III) Starch
(D) Cheese (IV) a gel

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:

questions.questionText: Hydrogen has three isotopes : protium (1H), deuterium (2H or D) and tritium (3H or T). They have nearly same chemical properties but different physical properties. They differ in :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following oxoacids of sulphur contains "S" in two different oxidation states?

questions.questionText: The drug tegamet is :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Correct statement about photo-chemical smog is :
options: (B) It is a mixture of smoke, fog and SO<sub>2</sub>.
(D) It results from reaction of unsaturated hydrocarbons.

questions.questionText:
In the metallurgical extraction of copper, following reaction is used :

FeO + SiO2 $$\to$$ FeSiO3

FeO and FeSiO3 respectively are.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Among the given oxides of nitrogen ; N2O, N2O3, N2O4 and N2O5, the number of compound/(s) having N - N bond is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements, one is Assertion (A) and other is Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : Natural rubber is a linear polymer of isoprene called cis-polyisoprene with elastic properties.

Reason (R) : The cis-polyisoprene molecules consist of various chains held together by strong polar interactions with coiled structure.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct one from the options given below :
options: (A) Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
(B) Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
100 g of an ideal gas is kept in a cylinder of 416 L volume at 27$$^\circ$$C under 1.5 bar pressure. The molar mass of the gas is __________ g mol$$-$$1. (Nearest integer)

(Given : R = 0.083 L bar K$$-$$1 mol$$-$$1)
